Formula / Description / How it is comput: Takes `alaram_id` from the Excel file or generates dynamically using `INC-{datetime.utcnow()}`. Frontend renders `incident.incident_id || incident.alaram_id`.
Extracted directly from the `device` column of the uploaded Excel sheet.
Extracted directly from the `resource_name` or `resource_type` column of the uploaded Excel sheet.
Rendered as 'Last 15 min from {incident.timestamp}' to show the time window used for fetching logs and metrics.
Takes the very first log from the aggregated `incident.logs[0]` array, which represents the root trigger alarm.
A list of logs (`incident.logs`) fetched from the logging system (or mock data) that occurred within the 15-minute correlation window prior to the trigger event.
API / External Call: Logging System Search API (e.g., Elasticsearch, Loki)

Formula / Description / How it is comput: Displayed statically as 'Critical' on the UI for all incidents in the playground.
Generated dynamically by the backend (`incident.goal`) to direct the agentic pipeline on what to solve.

Fetches telemetry (`incident.signals`) for the device. The frontend maps internal metric names like `utilization_percent` to readable names like 'Bandwidth Utilization' and units like '%' using `KPI_MAP`.
The `id` of the intent that achieved the highest combined score. Chosen from a list of predefined intents after evaluating rules and ML logic.
Evaluates the Intent Score: if >0.85 it shows '🟢 Strong match', if >0.65 shows '🟡 Medium match', else '🔴 Weak match'.
Computed in backend via `compute_final_confidence` combining `0.5 * rule_score(logs/metrics weighting) + 0.5 * ml_model.predict_proba`.
API / External Call: ML Model API or Internal Inference Engine

Formula / Description / How it is comput: Takes all evaluated intents, sorts them descending by score, and strips out the top 3 (excluding the best intent) to display as backups. Shows alternate routes the router considered.

For the selected intent, displays the exact metrics and threshold operators (e.g., `util_5m > 90` with weight) that evaluated to true in the `rule_based_intent_detection` loop. Shows WHY the router picked this intent.
List of specific string keywords defined in the intent that were successfully pattern-matched inside the aggregated 15-minute `incident.logs`. Shows WHY the router picked this intent.

A plain text explanation of what the chosen intent actually means conceptually (e.g. 'This intent triggers when sudden throughput drops heavily correlate with packet loss'). Makes the intent mathematically legible to humans.
Simply fetches the intent ID chosen in the previous step (`incident.intent`) to maintain context.
Selects the hypothesis (`topHypo.description`) that obtained the highest calculated score. The score is displayed as a percentage: `Math.round(total_score * 100)`.
Calculates `total_score = signal_score(metric rules) + log_score(log keyword matches)` for every hypothesis under the Selected Intent, mapping ID to score.
For every evaluated hypothesis, maps the specific raw log strings from `incident.logs` that triggered a pattern match (`h.matched_logs`) to prove why the hypothesis got a log_score.
Displays the unique ID assigned to the semantic card (`sitObj.situation_id`). Extracted from DB or generated.
Finds a situation template matching `template_id`. Uses regex to replace `{var}` placeholders with actual values of `device`, `top_hypothesis`, metrics etc., yielding a human-readable summary.
Statically displays 'N/A' on the UI as tags are not populated yet.
Generates a raw JSON payload combining metadata generated by the situation logic and all logs: `JSON.stringify({...sitObj.metadata, logs: incident.logs})`.
Statically displays 'Vector representation stored for similarity search'. On the backend, this text is generated into a vector array for DB insertion.
API / External Call: Vector DB embedding generation model API (e.g. OpenAI ada or local)

Formula / Description / How it is comput: Passes `incident.situation_card.situation_text` as the query string parameter to search the historical store.
Extracts `hCases[0].case_id`, which represents the historical case holding the highest cosine similarity score to the current query.
Loops through the retrieved historical matches mapping their past `case_id`, `sit_summary`, `rca`, and `remedy`. Converts `sim_score` to a display percentage (`sim_score * 100`).
Statically renders '+0.15' on the UI to indicate the positive weight applied to the hypothesis score due to historical backing.
Extracts the `rca` key parsed directly from the JSON returned by the Large Language Model prompt (which contained all previous evidence).
Extracts the `confidence` key outputted by the LLM as part of its judgment reasoning.
Formats the list of objects returned by the LLM under `evidence_used` joining `metric` and `value` to display what specific telemetry the LLM based its RCA upon.

Parses the list of strings output by the LLM under `remedy`, replacing semicolons with line breaks and bullets for formatting (`r.replace(';', '
• ')`).
